summary: All 30 criteria passed. The sprint delivers a well-structured test infrastructure foundation: run.sh is a clean entry point with prerequisite checks, --filter support, and TTY-aware output. test-helper.bash provides a comprehensive set of helper functions (setup/teardown, init_test_repo, source_harness_lib, install_fixture, assert_json_field, run_hook). All 19 fixture files are valid JSON with the correct structure. The meta-tests/ directory is fully independent from the existing tests/ directory. No regressions to check (regressionSprints is empty).

holistic.correctness: 10/10 -- All 30 criteria pass without qualification. Every feature in the contract is implemented exactly as specified.
holistic.completeness: 10/10 -- All three features (F1: Test Runner, F2: Test Helper Library, F3: Fixture Files) are fully implemented with no gaps.
holistic.codeQuality: 9/10 -- Clean idiomatic bash with set -euo pipefail, proper quoting, clear comments, and logical organization. The only minor nit: HARNESS_STATE is set as a relative path which works but could be fragile if cd is called later in tests.
holistic.robustness: 8/10 -- Prerequisite checks with helpful install instructions, handles both --filter and --filter= syntaxes, TTY detection for output format. run_hook captures exit codes properly. Could improve by validating fixture argument existence in install_fixture.
holistic.cliUx: 8/10 -- Good error messages for missing prereqs, usage help on unknown arguments, timing output. TAP vs pretty detection is a nice touch.
holistic.maintainability: 9/10 -- Well-organized fixture directory covering all major harness data types. Helper functions are composable and will serve future sprint test files well. Clear separation between infrastructure and test content.
